message:
feat(abi): delegate mntent line parsing/serialization to core (bd-mn-2, closes bd-mn-epic)

Refactors frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs to delegate the byte-level
mntent logic to the new pure-safe frankenlibc_core::mntent module:

  - hasmntopt: was ~12 lines of inline windowed comma-token scan;
    now: one call to core::mntent::has_mnt_opt(), Some(off) -> ptr.add(off).

  - getmntent_r: was ~85 lines of inline whitespace splitting,
    blank/comment skipping, freq/passno parsing, plus 4x manually-
    inlined copy_nonoverlapping/NUL packing; now: parse_mntent_line()
    plus a small `pack` closure that handles each of the 4 fields
    uniformly.

  - addmntent: was CStr -> String::to_string_lossy -> format!() macro;
    now: build core::mntent::MntFields directly from CStrs, call
    format_mntent_line() into a Vec<u8>, write_all().

The C-ABI marshalling — struct mntent field-pointer writes (4
*mut c_char + 2 c_int packed at the well-known offsets), caller-
buffer NUL packing, BufReader read_until/Write all integration —
stays at the boundary, where it has to be.

Verified:
  - tests/unistd_abi_test.rs mnt*: 2/2 pass (mntent_reads_proc_mounts,
    setmntent_nonexistent_returns_null)
  - tests/unistd_abi_test.rs (full): 178/178 pass

Closes bd-mn-epic. The mntent line parser is now reachable from any
caller (not just getmntent_r) and exhaustively unit-tested in
isolation behind #![deny(unsafe_code)].
